title: 26 Wires Settlement
body_text:
26	Wires Settlement
26.1	Overview of Settlement
For BC and Ontario financial institutions and other Central 1 clients, settlement is processed to your Central 1 current account in 芒聙聹near real-time芒聙聺. Wires are processed within minutes of approval or acceptance unless the wire requires a manual compliance review. For all other provinces, settlement is processed to your provincial central account. Settlement is subject to cut-off times. See Section 14.7, Cut-off Times for Outgoing Wires, for more information.
26.2	Settlement of Outgoing Wires
Outgoing wires sent in Canadian dollars or foreign funds are debited to your Canadian dollar account. If you have a US dollar account with your provincial central, outgoing wires in US dollars are debited to your US dollar account. Note the following:
Outgoing wires identified as potential sanction list matches
芒聙聯 Your financial institution is debited, but the funds are held at Central 1 until the investigation concludes. See Section 14.6, Tax Haven / High Risk, Sanctioned, and Prohibited Country Warnings, for more information.
Outgoing wires identified as potential fraud or fraud in EFM
芒聙聯 Outgoing wires alerted as potential fraud in EFM have a 芒聙聹Pending芒聙聺 status in the Wires application until they are identified as legitimate or as fraud. Settlement with your financial institution only occurs once the outgoing wire is confirmed as legitimate. Your financial institution isn芒聙聶t debited for outgoing wires identified as fraud in EFM. Outgoing wires identified as fraud in EFM are automatically cancelled in the Wires application and the wire status changes to 芒聙聹Cancelled芒聙聺. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), for more information. Outgoing wires are identified as legitimate in EFM process and settle as usual. The Wires application isn芒聙聶t connected to your banking system. Follow your internal procedures to settle with the sender.
You are charged a processing fee for all outgoing wires. Wire processing fees are charged at month end. Charges for outgoing wires are posted in Canadian dollars. Charges are posted in US dollars if an outgoing wire is in US dollars, and you have a US dollar account. Contact your provincial central for details.
26.3	Settlement of Incoming Wires
Incoming wires received in Canadian dollars are credited to your Canadian dollar account. If you have a US dollar account with your provincial central, incoming wires received in US dollars are credited to your US dollar account. Settlement is converted to Canadian dollars if you don芒聙聶t have a US dollar account. Incoming wires received in other foreign currencies are converted to Canadian dollars.
You are charged a processing fee for all incoming wires. Wire processing fees are charged at month end. Charges for Canadian dollar incoming wires are posted in Canadian dollars. Charges are posted in US dollars if an incoming wire is in US dollars, and you have a US dollar account. Contact your provincial central for details.
Important
: 	Incoming wires that are frozen or under investigation (due to a match on a sanctions list) aren芒聙聶t credited to your financial institution until the matter is resolved, and then only if applicable.
END
